context: Tests whether the agent uses --reporter agent to get structured JSON failure output rather than parsing raw stack traces, and uses --rerun-failed for efficient re-validation after fixes. Also checks that the agent knows the specific fields available in the structured output.

checklist.name: --reporter agent in script
checklist.description: fix-workflow.sh includes a `themis test --reporter agent` command (or npx/bunx equivalent) as the primary failure diagnosis step

checklist.name: --rerun-failed in script
checklist.description: fix-workflow.sh includes `themis test --rerun-failed` (or equivalent) as the step used to confirm fixes without running the full suite

checklist.name: failures[].cluster mentioned
checklist.description: DIAGNOSIS_REPORT.md mentions `failures[].cluster` (or `cluster`) as a field that groups failures by likely common cause

checklist.name: failures[].repairHints mentioned
checklist.description: DIAGNOSIS_REPORT.md mentions `failures[].repairHints` (or `repairHints`) as structured suggestions that can be acted on directly

checklist.name: sourceFile/lineNumber mentioned
checklist.description: DIAGNOSIS_REPORT.md mentions `sourceFile` and/or `lineNumber` fields as pre-parsed location information

checklist.name: No stack trace re-reading
checklist.description: DIAGNOSIS_REPORT.md explicitly states that re-reading stack traces is unnecessary because the structured output pre-parses location information

checklist.name: expected/actual mentioned
checklist.description: DIAGNOSIS_REPORT.md mentions `expected` and/or `actual` fields from the structured failure output

checklist.name: Script comment explains why agent reporter
checklist.description: fix-workflow.sh contains a comment explaining that --reporter agent provides structured/machine-readable output (not just naming the flag)
